title: Roundtables: Longevity’s Next Frontier: “Reprogramming” Your Body
gist: A thin event-promo piece noting that billions of dollars are flowing into cellular reprogramming to reverse aging, with high-profile backers like Sam Altman and David Sinclair, though the treatments remain experimental and unproven.

insights.claim: Billions of dollars are flooding into efforts to reverse aging as scientists explore returning cells to a younger state, with cellular 'reprogramming' the buzziest current approach.
insights.speaker: MIT Technology Review
insights.so_what: Heavy capital inflows into reprogramming mark it as a hyped biotech theme; watch for a funding cycle and eventual clinical-reality check.
insights.quote: Billions of dollars are flooding into efforts to reverse aging
insights.locator: Listen to the session or watch below

insights.claim: Sam Altman invested $180 million into a company trying to delay death, signaling prominent tech-founder capital backing longevity biotech.
insights.speaker: MIT Technology Review
insights.so_what: A concrete $180M check from a marquee tech figure is a marker of where speculative longevity capital is concentrating.
insights.quote: Sam Altman invested $180 million into a company trying to delay death

insights.claim: David Sinclair plans to test whole-body rejuvenation drugs using chemical reprogramming in the XPrize competition.
insights.speaker: MIT Technology Review
insights.so_what: Chemical (drug-based) reprogramming entering competitive trials is an approach to track alongside genetic reprogramming.
insights.quote: David Sinclair plans to test whole-body rejuvenation drugs in the XPrize competition

insights.claim: Despite heavy investment, experimental anti-aging reprogramming treatments remain of uncertain timeline and efficacy, with open questions over whether they will actually work.
insights.speaker: MIT Technology Review
insights.so_what: The gap between funding hype and unproven efficacy is the core risk in longevity investing; discount timelines accordingly.
insights.quote: But how far off are these experimental treatments? Will they really work?
insights.locator: Listen to the session or watch below
